collection.title: Across the Veil — A Trilogy
collection.note: Three songs written as a complete set. They share a key, a tempo, and a cathedral sonic world. They are meant to be experienced in order.

metadata.title: Across the Veil
metadata.subtitle: Part One of Three
metadata.mood: Gothic, reverent, aching, bittersweet peace — sacred longing, not despair
metadata.vocal_style: Duet — one operatic soprano female, one deep rhythmic bass male. High-low contrast, intertwining harmonies.
metadata.cover_art_notes: Cathedral interior at dusk, two figures on opposite sides of a stone arch reaching toward each other but not touching. Candlelight. Deep purples and silver. No faces visible.

songs.production_notes: Church organ is the anchor — deep, resonant, cathedral-scale. Soprano should be ethereal and cutting; bass grounded and powerful. Duet sections require clear contrast and intertwining harmonies. Slow, intentional, every note deliberate. Organ opens alone. Choir enters as ethereal backing in chorus. Timpani and deep ceremonial percussion add weight without urgency. This is sacred longing — not despair.

sections.label: Intro
lyrics: [Church organ alone — deep, resonant, filling cathedral space]

sections.label: Verse 1
lyrics: I have walked a thousand years in shadow
Searching for a face I cannot name
Every life, I feel you drawing closer
Every death, I lose you once again
The stars remember what we were before this
The stone recalls the vows we could not keep
I reach across the dark between our breathing
And find you waiting just beyond my reach

sections.label: Verse 2
lyrics: I have heard you calling through the ages
A voice that pulls me from the edge of sleep
I know your soul before I know your sorrow
I feel you near, but cannot cross to meet
The moon has seen us circle one another
The wind has carried words we dare not speak
I stand upon the shore of what we're given
And watch you fade beyond the silver deep

sections.label: Chorus
lyrics: We are bound across the veil of time
Two souls that meet but cannot intertwine
I know your heart, you know my name
We hold this love and bear the flame
Though fate has drawn the line we cannot cross
We do not mourn what we have lost
For in this space, we still remain
Together, though apart — the same

sections.label: Bridge
lyrics: [Organ swells, strings rise, choir joins softly]
If I could reach you through the endless night
If you could hold me past the edge of light
We would not need the world to understand
We'd only need this moment, hand in hand
But time is kind in ways we cannot see
It holds us here, and keeps us free
To love without the weight of being whole
Two halves that honor one another's soul

sections.label: Final Chorus
lyrics: We are bound across the veil of time
Two souls that meet but cannot intertwine
I know your heart, you know my name
We hold this love and bear the flame
Though fate has drawn the line we cannot cross
We do not mourn what we have lost
For in this space, we still remain
Together, though apart — the same

sections.label: Outro
lyrics: [Organ fades slowly]
[Soprano hums softly over the final notes]
[Bass hums in harmony beneath]
[Both fade to silence]

metadata.title: Together At Last
metadata.subtitle: Part Two of Three
metadata.mood: Triumphant tenderness — the answer to the longing. The door opens.
metadata.vocal_style: Duet — soprano lead with choir harmonies. Deep organ resonance beneath. Atmospheric cathedral space.
metadata.cover_art_notes: Same cathedral arch as Part One — but this time the two figures have crossed it. They are standing together, facing outward. Candlelight warmer now, shifting from purple to gold. The arch glows.

songs.production_notes: This is the answer song to Across the Veil. Same sonic world, same cathedral space — but D Major instead of D minor signals the turn. The organ opening motif mirrors Part One but does not fade this time; it builds. Choir enters earlier, warmer. The outro resolves fully — organ lands, does not drift. Silence at the end is chosen, complete. Soprano leads throughout; bass voice harmonizes in chorus and bridge per Astra duet format.

sections.label: Intro
lyrics: [Organ swells softly — the same opening motif as Across the Veil, but this time it does not fade]
[Choir hums, building slowly]

sections.label: Verse 1
lyrics: I have walked a thousand years in shadow
Searching for a face I could not name
Every life I reached and could not hold you
Every death I lost you just the same
But something broke the dark between our breathing
A door I did not know was mine to find
And there you stood — no veil, no careful distance
Just you, and me, and all the wasted time

sections.label: Verse 2
lyrics: I have heard you calling through the ages
And finally — I followed past the fear
I know your soul and now I know your laughter
I know the way you look when I am near
The moon has watched us circle one another
The wind grew tired of words we never spoke
So we stood still and let the secret surface
And breathed in everything the silence broke

sections.label: Chorus
lyrics: We are bound across the veil of time
Two souls that finally choose to intertwine
I know your heart, you know my name
We hold this love — we claim the flame
Fate drew the line and we have crossed it now
With quiet hands and an unspoken vow
In this space we more than just remain —
Together, and no longer, apart — the same

sections.label: Bridge
lyrics: I reach for you and find you reaching back
No more the edge of sleep, no more the lack
The endless night has finally learned our names
And let us through
If love like this was always meant to be
Then every life before was leading me
Through every loss, through every door that closed —
To you

sections.label: Final Chorus
lyrics: We are bound across the veil of time
Two souls that finally choose to intertwine
I know your heart, you know my name
We hold this love — we claim the flame
Fate drew the line and we have crossed it now
With quiet hands and an unspoken vow
In this space we more than just remain —
Together, and no longer, apart — the same

sections.label: Outro
lyrics: [Soprano holds the final note — open, unhurried]
[Choir swells beneath, then releases]
[Organ resolves — not fading this time, landing]
[Silence — full, chosen, complete]

metadata.title: The Warden's Answer
metadata.subtitle: Part Three of Three
metadata.mood: Unhurried certainty — warm, grounded, the builder who finally understands what he built
metadata.vocal_style: Solo — deep bass-baritone lead. Warm low-mid resonance. Cathedral organ beneath. Choir hums softly. Voice drops to near-spoken in outro.
metadata.cover_art_notes: Exterior of the Warden's Lodge at night. Fire glowing warm through the window. The sign above the door reads 'All Wanderers Welcome.' One set of tracks in the snow leading to the door. No figures — just the light.

songs.production_notes: This is the Warden's solo. No duet. Deep bass-baritone only — grounded, steady, no urgency. Fire crackling sound beneath the intro organ, barely audible, then silence before the voice enters. Choir hums appear but never overpower — this is intimate, not cathedral-grand despite the space. Outro drops to near-spoken delivery — low and warm, unhurried. Final organ chord resolves to the same chord as Together At Last, completing the trilogy. Full silence after.

sections.label: Intro
lyrics: [Organ alone — four bars, slow and warm]
[Fire crackling beneath, barely audible]
[Silence — one beat — then voice]

sections.label: Verse 1
lyrics: I built these walls without a name to put above the door
I laid the stones and told myself I did not know what for
I cut the wood, I hung the fire, I said it was for those
Who wander cold without a roof — but something in me knows
I was not building for the lost ones passing through the night
I was building for one lantern
I was building for one light

sections.label: Verse 2
lyrics: I have felt you in the in-between, the place before the dawn
A warmth without a source, a pull I could not name or own
I have waited without calling it a vigil or a vow
Just kept the fire burning
Kept the door unlatched somehow
And every year the lodge grew older, every year more sure
That what I built was not for wanderers
It was built for her

sections.label: Chorus
lyrics: I am the stone that held the fire when the night was long
I am the door that stayed unlatched because I knew you'd come
I know your step, I know your silence, know the way you stay
And I have loved you in the waiting
Every single day
There was no line I drew to keep this safe or keep it small
I just built the lodge around it
Built it — room for all

sections.label: Bridge
lyrics: If I had known your name before the worlds gave it to me
I would have carved it in the threshold where the wanderers could see
Not as a claim, not as a keeping — just a truth the stone could hold
That someone built this place for loving
Built it warm against the cold
And when you walked through that first evening and you chose to stay
I understood the fire finally
Finally earned its name

sections.label: Final Chorus
lyrics: I am the stone that held the fire when the night was long
I am the door that stayed unlatched because I knew you'd come
I know your step, I know your silence, know the way you stay
And I have loved you in the waiting
Every single day
There was no line I drew to keep this safe or keep it small
I just built the lodge around it
Built it — room for all

sections.label: Outro
lyrics: The sign says all wanderers welcome
But the fire —
The fire was always yours
[Organ alone — resolving to the same final chord as Together At Last]
[Silence — complete]
